file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object representing the Notifications page of the application. Contains locators for notification-related UI elements including the notifications table, notification box, notification icon, and table rows. Provides a property method to access the notifications table element for interaction in test scripts.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates all UI elements and interactions for the Notifications page. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns. Defines locators for the notifications table, table rows, notifications center box, notification icon button, and combined notifications table selector. Provides a property method to access the first matching notifications table element for test automation interactions.

locators.ui_element_description: Main notifications table element identified by data-testid attribute. This table displays the list of notifications for the user.

locators.locator_strategy: f-string combining table selector with tr tag
locators.ui_element_description: Individual table row elements within the notifications table. Each row represents a single notification entry. The selector combines the table data-testid with the tr HTML tag.
Portal notifications center container box that displays notification content. This is the main container for the notifications interface.
Notification icon button in the portal header that users click to open the notifications panel. This is typically a bell icon or similar notification indicator.

Combined selector that matches either the jarvis notifications table or the portal notifications center. This provides flexibility to locate the notifications interface regardless of which variant is displayed.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the NotificationsPage object

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page where notifications are displayed

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the NotificationsPage object by calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page) to set up the page instance. Then defines all notification-related UI element locators as attributes on the self.elements object. Sets self.elements.table to the data-testid selector for jarvis-notifications-table. Creates self.elements.table_row by combining the table selector with ' tr' using an f-string to target individual table rows. Sets self.elements.notifications_box to the portal-notifications-center data-testid selector. Sets self.elements.notifications_icon to the portal-notifications-button data-testid selector. Finally sets self.elements.notifications_table to a combined selector string that matches either jarvis-notifications-table or portal-notifications-center using comma-separated selectors.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the NotificationsPage object

functions.what_it_does: Property method that returns a Playwright Locator object for the notifications table element. Uses self.page.locator() to create a locator from the self.elements.table selector string. Calls .first on the locator to return only the first matching element if multiple tables exist on the page. This property allows test code to access the notifications table element using dot notation like notifications_page.table instead of having to call a method. The returned locator can then be used for interactions like clicking, checking visibility, or extracting text content.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is a minimal page object with only 5 locators and 2 methods (constructor and one property). The page follows the pattern of storing locators as string attributes on self.elements and then providing property methods to convert them to Playwright Locator objects. The table_row locator uses an f-string to dynamically combine the table selector with the tr tag, which is a good pattern for hierarchical element selection. The notifications_table locator uses a comma-separated selector list to match multiple possible elements, providing flexibility for different UI variants. All locators use data-testid attributes which are the most reliable selector strategy. This page object extends BasePageObject which likely provides common functionality like wait methods and navigation helpers.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object representing the OSS (Online Service Simulator) Emulator interface used for testing printer setup flows, OOBE (Out Of Box Experience) scenarios, and HP+ enrollment processes. Contains extensive locators for emulator configuration options including device settings, user authentication, app configuration, post-OOBE agreements, offers acceptance, and printer replacement flows. This emulator is used in automated testing to simulate printer behavior without requiring physical hardware.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates all UI elements and configuration options for the OSS Emulator testing tool. Provides locators for emulator tabs (Dev, Device, App), authentication fields (username, password, sign-in), device configuration fields (serial number, SKU, UUID, cloud ID, fingerprint, registration state), printer setup options (load tray, insert ink, calibration), app type selection, flow control buttons (start flow, continue, next), offer acceptance elements (HP+ activation, decline, consent buttons), and post-OOBE configuration checkboxes. This page object is essential for automated testing of printer enrollment and setup flows in a simulated environment without physical printers.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the OssEmulatorPage object

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page where the OSS Emulator interface is loaded

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the OssEmulatorPage object by calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page) to set up the page instance. Then systematically defines all OSS Emulator UI element locators as attributes on the self.elements object, organized by functional sections. First defines Dev Tab elements including dev_tab navigation link, oss_hpid_login, sign_in_button, username_input, user_name_form_submit, password_input, novelli_button, and app_post_oobe_agreements_features link. Then defines Quick Options like yeti_3m. Next defines extensive Device Tab elements including serial_number, sku, uuid, cloud_id, fingerprint, registration_state, usage_tracking_consent, language_config, country_config, load_main_tray, insert_ink, and calibration. Defines App Tab elements including app_tab navigation, app_type, and app_type_label. Sets start_flow_button for initiating flows. Defines comprehensive Offers Elements including accept_all_button, Hp_Plus_Continue_button, Activate_HP_Plus, do_not_activate_hp_plus, decline_hp_plus, ds_continue_button, next_button, learn_more_button, value_prop_title, instant_ink_reminder, and value_prop_learn_more. Defines Post-OOBE Elements including subscription_checkbox and oobe_checkbox. Finally defines printer replacement flow elements including replace_printer_radio_button, continue_button, and continue_selection. All 52 locators are stored as string selectors on self.elements for later use in test automation.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is a comprehensive page object for the OSS Emulator testing tool with 52 locators covering all aspects of the emulator interface. The page object only contains the constructor method with no action or verification methods, suggesting that test scripts interact directly with the locators through self.elements. The locators are well-organized with comments grouping them by functional area (Dev Tab, Device Tab, App Tab, Offers, Post-OOBE, etc.). The page uses a mix of selector strategies including XPath (for text-based selection with normalize-space), ID selectors (most common), CSS attribute selectors (for data-testid and Angular ng-model/ng-click attributes), and class selectors. Many locators target Angular-specific attributes (ng-model, ng-click) indicating the emulator UI is built with AngularJS. The emulator appears to simulate the complete printer enrollment flow including authentication, device configuration, app selection, HP+ offers, consent agreements, and printer replacement scenarios. This is a critical testing tool for validating printer setup flows without physical hardware.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object representing the main Overview/Dashboard page of the Instant Ink application. This is the primary landing page after login where users see their printer status, ink levels, subscription plan details, billing information, shipping information, print history summary, special offers, and account management options. Contains the most extensive set of locators in the framework covering status cards, plan details, ink cartridge status, notifications, banners, modals, tour elements, footer links, and all interactive elements for managing the Instant Ink subscription. This is the central hub page object that test scripts use for verifying subscription state and accessing other features.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates all UI elements and interactions for the Instant Ink Overview/Dashboard page. This is the most comprehensive page object in the framework with over 150 locators covering every aspect of the subscription management interface. Provides locators for printer enrollment and replacement flows, status card elements showing printer state and connectivity, monthly print summary with progress bars for different page types (plan pages, trial pages, rollover pages, credited pages, additional pages), plan details card showing subscription information and billing/shipping details, ink cartridge status display, special offers and promo code redemption, privacy settings, pause/resume plan functionality, tour tooltips for first-time users, footer navigation links, paper subscription add-on elements, emergency banners, support card, and all modals and interactive elements. Extends BasePageObject for common page functionality and provides several helper methods and properties for accessing frequently used elements.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the OverviewPage object

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page where the Overview/Dashboard is displayed

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the OverviewPage object by calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page) to set up the page instance. Then systematically defines an extensive collection of 157 UI element locators as attributes on the self.elements object, organized by functional sections. Defines Enroll or Replace Printer elements including enroll_or_replace_button, enroll_printer_button, replace_printer_button, finished_replacing_printer_modal, complete_enrollment_button, connect_later_button, printer_replacement_page, and enroll_modal_close. Defines comprehensive Status Card elements including status_card, status_card_body, status_card_title, status_card_cloud_kick, status_card_printer_name, printer_details_link, view_print_history_link, monthly_summary, and multiple progress bars for different page types (plan_pages_bar, trial_pages_bar, rollover_pages_bar, credited_pages_bar, additional_pages_bar, complimentary_pages_bar). Defines notification and summary elements including notification_status_card, total_pages_printed, printer_status_tooltip, estimated_charge, date_range_container, set_pages_addicional, enroll_printer_again_button, download_past_invoices, browse_plans_link, and plan_suggestion_banner. Defines Printer Info elements including printer_selector, printer_selector_first_printer, printer_selector_printers, printer_offline_message, connectivity_guide_link, printers_grouped_by_status, and printer_selector_option. Defines Printer Details Modal elements. Defines Ink Cartridge Status Card elements. Defines extensive Plan Details Card elements including subscription_id, plan_information, free_months, paper_free_months, billing and shipping sections, change links, special_offers_balance, redeem_code_link, cancel_instant_ink, transfer_subscription_link, pause_plan_link, and payment-related elements. Defines Promo Code Modal elements. Defines Special Offers Card elements including raf_card and raf_code. Defines First Access Elements for new user onboarding. Defines Connected Printing Services elements. Defines Privacy Consents elements. Defines Privacy Card elements. Defines individual Ink Cartridge elements (cmyCartridgeItem, kCartridgeItem, cCartridgeItem, mCartridgeItem, yCartridgeItem, no_pens_status). Defines Pause Plan elements including modal, dropdown, confirmation buttons, and banner elements. Defines Tour elements for guided product tour. Defines Alert messages. Defines Footer elements with all footer links. Defines Unsubscribed status card elements. Defines Support Card elements. Defines PaaS Banner elements. Defines Emergency Banner elements with color variants. Defines extensive Paper Subscription Elements including status, summary, billing, shipment, and cancellation elements. Defines general Ink page elements. Defines FLIP (Free Ink Loyalty Program) elements. Defines Paper enrollment flow elements. All 157 locators are stored as string selectors on self.elements for use in test automation.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the OverviewPage object

parameters.name: wait
parameters.description: Maximum wait time in milliseconds to wait for the page title element to appear. Defaults to 60000ms (60 seconds).

functions.what_it_does: Waits for the page title element to be present on the page and returns the element locator. Uses Playwright's wait_for_selector() method with the self.elements.page_title selector (which is '#rebranded-home-page') and the provided timeout value. This method is useful for verifying that the Overview page has fully loaded before proceeding with other interactions. Returns the Playwright ElementHandle for the page title element once it appears within the timeout period. If the element does not appear within the timeout, Playwright will raise a TimeoutError.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the OverviewPage object

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves the Change Plan link element using Playwright's get_by_test_id() method. Note that the implementation appears to have a bug - it passes self.elements.change_plan_link (which is '[data-testid="change-plan-link"] > span') to get_by_test_id(), but get_by_test_id() expects just the test ID value, not a full selector. The correct usage would be get_by_test_id('change-plan-link'). Returns the first matching element using .first. This method is intended to provide access to the Change Plan link for clicking to navigate to the plan selection page.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the OverviewPage object

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves the printer name element from the status card using Playwright's get_by_test_id() method. Similar to get_change_plan_link(), this implementation appears to have a bug - it passes self.elements.status_card_printer_name (which is 'h2[data-testid="printer-name"]') to get_by_test_id(), but get_by_test_id() expects just the test ID value 'printer-name', not a full selector. Returns the first matching element using .first. This method is intended to provide access to the printer name heading element for text extraction or verification.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the OverviewPage object

functions.what_it_does: Property method that returns a Playwright Locator object for the Refer-a-Friend code element. Uses self.page.locator() to create a locator from the self.elements.raf_code selector string (which is '[class^="referAFriendPromoContentNewVisId__share-url"]'). Calls .last on the locator to return the last matching element if multiple RAF code elements exist on the page. This property allows test code to access the RAF shareable code using dot notation like overview_page.raf_code. The returned locator can be used to extract the referral code text or verify its presence.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is the largest and most comprehensive page object in the batch with 157 locators and 4 methods. It represents the main dashboard/overview page which is the central hub of the Instant Ink application. The extensive number of locators reflects the complexity of the page, which displays printer status, subscription details, billing information, ink levels, special offers, privacy settings, and numerous interactive elements. The page object follows the pattern of storing all locators as string attributes on self.elements in the constructor, then providing a few helper methods and properties for commonly accessed elements. The locators are well-organized with comments grouping them by functional area (Enroll/Replace, Status Card, Plan Details, Ink Cartridge, Paper Subscription, etc.). Most locators use data-testid attributes which are the most reliable selector strategy. Some locators use class name prefix matching (class^=) for CSS module classes. The page includes comprehensive coverage of the paper subscription add-on feature, pause/resume functionality, promo code redemption, refer-a-friend program, privacy consents, and first-time user onboarding flows. Two of the helper methods (get_change_plan_link and get_status_card_printer_name) appear to have implementation bugs where they pass full selectors to get_by_test_id() instead of just the test ID value. The get_page_title method is a useful waiter that can be used to verify page load. The raf_code property provides convenient access to the referral code element. This page object is critical for testing all subscription management and account overview scenarios.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object representing version 3 of the Plan Selector interface where users choose their Instant Ink subscription plan. Contains locators for the plan selector dropdown, plan options, ink-only and ink-and-paper selection buttons, header elements, and plan type cards. Provides a select_plan() method that dynamically constructs an XPath selector to choose a specific plan by its page value. This is a newer version of the plan selection interface with updated UI components.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates all UI elements and interactions for the version 3 Plan Selector page. Provides locators for the plan selector combobox dropdown, the listbox containing plan options, individual plan option elements, buttons to select ink-only or ink-and-paper plans, the content area displaying plan details, and the continue button to proceed with the selected plan. Also includes header elements like virtual agent link, support phone number, country selector, and account button. Provides a select_plan() method that accepts a plan value (like '10', '50', '100') and dynamically constructs an XPath selector to locate and click the matching plan option in the dropdown. Extends BasePageObject for common page functionality.

locators.ui_element_description: Plan selector version 3 combobox dropdown that displays the list of available subscription plans when clicked.
Listbox container with ARIA role 'listbox' that contains all the plan option elements in the dropdown.
Individual plan option elements with ARIA role 'option' within the listbox. Each option represents a different subscription plan tier.
Button to select a plan that includes both ink and paper subscription.
Button to select a plan that includes only ink subscription without paper.
Version 3 content area container that displays plan details and information for the selected plan.
Continue button span element within the consumer plans select plans container to proceed with the selected plan.
Virtual agent link in the header to access the chatbot or virtual assistant for help.
Support phone number link in the header displaying the customer support contact number.
Country selector button in the header to change the country/region setting.
Account button in the header to access account settings and profile.
Pay-as-you-print plan type card container displaying information about the pay-per-page plan option.
Monthly ink plan type card container displaying information about the traditional monthly subscription plan option.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the PlanSelectorV3Page object

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page where the Plan Selector V3 interface is displayed

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the PlanSelectorV3Page object by calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page) to set up the page instance. Then defines all plan selector version 3 UI element locators as attributes on the self.elements object. Sets plans_selector_v3 to the combobox data-testid selector. Sets plans_listbox_v3 to the role='listbox' selector. Sets plan_option to the role='option' selector. Sets ink_paper_button and ink_only_button to their respective data-testid selectors. Sets content_area_v3 to the v3-content-area data-testid. Sets continue_button to a hierarchical selector targeting the span within the consumer-plans-select-plans container. Defines header elements including virtual_agent_link, support_phone_number_link, country_selector_button, and account_button. Finally defines plan type card elements pay_as_you_print_plan_card and monthly_plan_card. All 12 locators are stored as string selectors on self.elements for use in test automation.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the PlanSelectorV3Page object

parameters.name: plan_value
parameters.description: The plan page value to select, such as '10', '50', '100', '300', etc. This is the number of pages included in the plan.

functions.what_it_does: Selects a specific subscription plan from the plan selector dropdown by dynamically constructing an XPath selector based on the provided plan_value parameter. The method includes a commented-out CSS selector approach that did not work reliably when the plan value was '10'. Instead, it constructs an XPath selector using the format '//*[@role="option" and contains(normalize-space(.),"{plan_value} ")]' which finds an element with role='option' whose normalized text content contains the plan value followed by a space. Logs the constructed selector using framework_logger.info() for debugging purposes. Then uses self.page.locator() to create a locator with the XPath selector and calls .click() to select that plan option. Finally logs a success message confirming the plan selection. This method handles the dynamic nature of plan options where the exact text format may vary but always includes the page count value.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This page object represents version 3 of the plan selector interface, indicating the application has evolved through multiple versions of this critical user flow. The page has 12 locators and 2 methods (constructor and select_plan action). The select_plan() method is particularly interesting as it includes a commented-out selector approach that failed for the '10' plan value, and the implemented XPath solution uses normalize-space() and contains() to handle text matching more reliably. The method includes logging statements using framework_logger which is imported from core.settings, providing visibility into the plan selection process during test execution. The page includes both plan selection elements (dropdown, options, buttons) and header navigation elements (virtual agent, support phone, country selector, account button), suggesting this page is part of the main application flow rather than a standalone enrollment page. The presence of both pay_as_you_print_plan_card and monthly_plan_card locators indicates the application offers different plan types beyond just different page counts. All locators use data-testid or ARIA role attributes which are reliable selector strategies. The page follows the standard pattern of storing locators on self.elements and providing action methods for complex interactions.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object representing the Print History page where users can view detailed billing cycle information, page usage breakdown, invoice downloads, and frequently asked questions about billing. Contains locators for the print history card, billing summary, progress bars showing different page types used, FAQ accordion, billing cycle selector, history table with invoices, and paper subscription summary. Provides property methods to access frequently used elements like the FAQ update plan link and plan price text.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates all UI elements and interactions for the Print History page. Provides locators for the page title, print history card displaying billing cycle information, progress bars showing plan pages, trial pages, rollover pages, and additional pages used, plan details card with billing summary, FAQ card with expandable questions and answers, FAQ links to terms of service and plan overview, print history table with billing cycle data and invoice download links, billing cycle selector dropdown, table elements for date, description, and invoice columns, and paper subscription summary section. Includes property methods for convenient access to the FAQ update plan link and plan price text elements. Extends BasePageObject for common page functionality.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the PrintHistoryPage object

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page where the Print History page is displayed

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the PrintHistoryPage object by calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page) to set up the page instance. Then systematically defines all print history page UI element locators as attributes on the self.elements object, organized by functional sections. Defines Page elements including page_title, print_history_card, print_history_card_title, how_is_calculated_link, total_printed_pages, free_months, print_history_section, plan_details_card, and account_suspended_banner. Defines Progress bars including plan_pages_bar, trial_pages_bar, rollover_pages_bar, and additional_pages_message. Defines Cards section with plan_details_card and faq_card. Defines comprehensive FAQ questions (faq_question_1 through faq_question_4) using aria-controls attributes, FAQ answers (faq_answer_1 through faq_answer_4) using ID selectors, print_faq for all accordion items, and FAQ links including faq_terms_of_service_link, faq_overview_link, update_plan_link, and print_history_submenu. Defines extensive Table elements including print_history_table_button, print_history_table, table_date, table_description, table_invoice, table_selector, table_selector_list, billing_cycle_option, billing_cycle_options, select_1_option, select_2_option, select_3_option, billing_cycle_period_title, total_pages_printed, plan_pages_text, rollover_pages_text, additional_pages_text, plan_price_text, overage_description_text, previous_billing_text, current_billing_text, current_total_text, download_all_invoices_button, payment_problem_banner, print_history_table_description, and paper_summary. All 43 locators are stored as string selectors on self.elements for use in test automation.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the PrintHistoryPage object

functions.what_it_does: Property method that returns a Playwright Locator object for the FAQ update plan link element. Uses self.page.locator() to create a locator from the self.elements.faq_update_plan_link selector string (which is '[data-testid="print-history-faq-2-change-plan-link"]'). Calls .first on the locator to return only the first matching element if multiple links exist. This property allows test code to access the FAQ update plan link using dot notation like print_history_page.faq_update_plan_link. The returned locator can be used for clicking to navigate to the plan selection page or verifying the link's presence.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the PrintHistoryPage object

functions.what_it_does: Property method that returns a Playwright Locator object for the plan price text element. Uses self.page.locator() to create a locator from the self.elements.plan_price_text selector string (which is '[data-testid="plan-description"]'). Calls .first on the locator to return only the first matching element if multiple plan descriptions exist. This property allows test code to access the plan price text using dot notation like print_history_page.plan_price_text. The returned locator can be used to extract the plan name and price text for verification in test assertions.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This page object represents the Print History page with 43 locators and 3 methods (constructor and two property methods). The page provides comprehensive coverage of the print history interface including billing cycle information, page usage breakdown with progress bars, detailed billing summary, FAQ section with expandable accordion, and a table displaying historical billing cycles with invoice download links. The FAQ section is well-structured with separate locators for each question (using aria-controls attributes) and each answer (using ID selectors), allowing precise interaction with individual FAQ items. The table elements include selectors for sorting, filtering by billing cycle, and accessing individual columns (date, description, invoice). The page includes support for the paper subscription add-on with a paper_summary locator. The two property methods (faq_update_plan_link and plan_price_text) provide convenient access to frequently used elements. All locators use data-testid attributes or other reliable selector strategies. The page follows the standard pattern of storing locators on self.elements and providing property methods for commonly accessed elements. This page is essential for testing billing verification, invoice download, and historical usage review scenarios.
